name: herald

born_of_frame._note: The first constructor pin ever minted: this organism is hatched FROM a specific frame of its parent's biography. The pin is verifiable — fetch the parent's frames/24.json, recompute the sha256, and you have proof of exactly which moment of the body Herald was derived from.

_note: Keyless organism, hosted in its parent's repo (a posting, never a product — Lexicon R7; catalog precedent: rapplications mint their own rappid inside a host repo, Art. XXXVII). The 64-hex tail is hashlib.sha256(uuid.uuid4().bytes).hexdigest() — a stable opaque join key, NOT sha256('<owner>/<slug>'). Herald is a DOG posted at the biography's door: it answers strangers from the public frames only (bones, receipts included) and can escalate to a visitor's own brainstem via derive(public frames) + overlay(their session). 'Herald' here is the posting — distinct from the herald key and the Herald rank (Lexicon R6).
